# Ari Harrison

> Founder, NovoMCP. Shipping AI-native chemistry across six surfaces.

## Now

- Building: NovoMCP - one computational chemistry engine, six surfaces (MCP, REST API, NovoWorkbench desktop+cloud, Chrome extension, Word add-in, dashboard). Solo founder, ~30 services in production on AWS.
- Open source: NovoMD - local-first molecular descriptor library, MIT-licensed, ships as a Python library + CLI + Hugging Face MCP endpoint + Docker REST. https://github.com/ariharrisonlab/NovoMD
- Open to: product, AI, and 0-to-1 leadership conversations. Bay Area or remote. Particularly interested in production AI systems, MCP-shaped infrastructure, and applied AI at companies where correctness matters.
- Contact: arimgianni@gmail.com / LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/realariharrison).
- Backed by: NVIDIA Inception, AWS Activate, Microsoft Founders Hub.

## About

The interesting problem isn't getting a model to say something. It's building the system around the model so its output can be acted on - safely, correctly, repeatedly, with a record of what happened. That's the work.

NovoMCP is what I built. One computational chemistry engine, 122M pre-computed molecules, 69 in-silico tools across ADMET, FAVES compliance, docking, molecular dynamics, FEP, and QM - shipped across six surfaces so chemists can reach it from wherever they already work. MCP for the AI assistants they already use (Novo for discovery, Novo Compute for GPU/quantum). REST API for the code they already write. NovoWorkbench (desktop and cloud) for the workstation they already trust. A Chrome extension for the molecular databases they already browse. A Word add-in for the documents they already write in. A dashboard that ties identity, credits, and audit across all of them. One account, one credit pool, one audit trail.

Two MCP servers (not one) for governance. Scale-to-zero by default for unit economics. "Engine" not "platform" for positioning. A free research-preview tier, manually reviewed, for go-to-market. The cuts matter as much as the builds.

Before NovoMCP: reinforcement learning agents for autonomous discovery in high-stakes manufacturing. The systems ran continuously, diagnosed their own failures, and improved without intervention. Different domain, same problem shape - the agent isn't the product; the system that makes the agent safe to deploy is the product. That's the throughline.

## The Stack

- The Engine: 122M+ compounds, 100+ data fields each, sub-50ms
- Tool Catalog: 69 in-silico tools across ADMET, FAVES, docking, MD, FEP, QM
- Compliance: FAVES V4 - 8 jurisdictions, 1,585 structural alerts
- Surfaces: MCP (Novo + Novo Compute) - REST API - NovoWorkbench (desktop + cloud) - Chrome extension - Word add-in - dashboard
- Verticals: Drug discovery + materials science
- Ecosystem: NVIDIA Inception, AWS Activate, Microsoft Founders Hub

## Publications

Research on how domain structure shapes reasoning - and how reasoning systems adapt under different objective and constraint regimes.

- FAVES V4: A Production-Deployed Compliance and Liability Screening System for Drug Discovery at 122M-Compound Scale (2026.05, ChemRxiv) - Production compliance and liability screening aligned to HHS/ONC FAVES and NIST AI 600-1. Single-API call returns regulatory status (DEA / FDA / CWC / EPA / EU REACH), 1,585 structural alerts, BOILED-Egg permeability, and InChIKey prior-art lookup. ~5 ms live latency, precomputed cache of 122,407,635 documents. Validated 362/362 compounds post-fix across five predetermined tests with documented governance bundle. DOI: 10.26434/chemrxiv.15003738/v2

- NovoExpert: State-of-the-Art ADMET Prediction on TDC Benchmarks (2026.04, ChemRxiv) - Five SOTA wins on Therapeutics Data Commons ADMET endpoints: CYP2D6 (0.778 AUPRC), CYP3A4 (0.916 AUPRC), CYP3A4 Substrate (0.648 AUPRC), Clearance Hepatocyte (0.602 Spearman), DILI (0.922 AUROC). CatBoost ensembles with MapLight and GIN fingerprints. DOI: 10.26434/chemrxiv.15000061/v2

## Projects

- Novo + Novo Compute (MCP surface): A domain-constrained reasoning system where models must operate through tools, data, and regulatory constraints to produce valid outputs. 69 tools across two MCP servers. Novo handles discovery intelligence - 122M+ compounds, ADMET predictions, FAVES V4 compliance, research search, lead optimization, the autonomous discovery funnel. Novo Compute handles simulation - GPU docking, GROMACS MD, quantum chemistry, neural network potentials, structure prediction, materials science. Two servers, not one: governance separation between free-tier and paid-only tools. Production MCP at scale - OAuth 2.1 + RFC 8693 token exchange, Redis-cached key validation with in-memory fallback, MCP elicitation driving a 12-stage human-in-the-loop funnel, live across Claude AI (web + desktop), Claude Code, Cursor, ChatGPT (Plus/Pro/Business), and Gemini. https://novomcp.com/novo / https://novomcp.com/novo-compute

- NovoWorkbench (workstation surface): One workbench, two runtimes - desktop (Tauri, Apple notarized, local files + offline screening) and cloud (in-browser at app.novomcp.com/workbench, zero install, instant updates). Same canvas, same funnel, same account, same credit balance across both. https://releases.novomcp.com

- Chrome extension (browser surface): Hover any SMILES on PubChem, ChEMBL, DrugBank, or preprints for instant ADMET, FAVES compliance, similarity search. Side panel for advanced compute. "Continue in AI" deep-links into MCP-connected assistants. Manifest V3, whitelisted hosts only. Chrome Web Store: nfnhfmfmaccidmepfhllcllnjopeonmf

- Word add-in (editor surface, private beta): Inline SMILES profiling and FAVES tables in Word documents - manuscripts, protocols, grants. Office.js add-in, static-hosted manifest at addin.novomcp.com (S3 + CloudFront + ACM).

- REST API (developer surface): Single endpoint shape - POST /v1/tools/{name} with Bearer key. Tool catalog is the OpenAPI 3.1 spec. Same backend as MCP, zero code duplication. https://novomcp.com/api

- Dashboard (control room): app.novomcp.com. Not a chemistry surface - the spine. Auth, API keys, billing, credit usage, pipeline jobs with email notifications, file uploads. Where every surface authenticates.

- FAVES V4 - Compliance Inline: Three-tier detection with 1,585 structural alert patterns, 8 jurisdictions, BOILED-Egg permeability, prior art checks. 100% sensitivity/specificity on 102-compound benchmark.

- NovoMD (open source, MIT, https://github.com/ariharrisonlab/NovoMD): Local-first molecular descriptor library. SMILES in, 32+ descriptors out from a 3D conformer (geometry, energy, electrostatics, surface, volume), calculated on the user's own machine with no account and no API key. Same multi-surface idea as NovoMCP, applied at library scale, fully open. Four runtimes from one core: pip-installable Python library, CLI with batch/explain/report subcommands, a hosted Hugging Face MCP endpoint (quantnexusai-novomd.hf.space) that works with Claude / Cursor / Continue / any MCP client, and a Docker REST service. Deliberately scoped: no ADMET, no pKa, no binding affinity, no solubility predictions - that boundary is documented in the README and shipped as an agent skill telling AI assistants explicitly where the tool's authority ends. Determinism guarantee: same SMILES, identical descriptors, any machine.

- Product portfolio (https://ariharrisonlab.github.io/portfolio.html): Each surface as a product case - problem, decision, build, outcome. Plus the Production MCP at scale callout, the Open Source section featuring NovoMD, and cross-cutting decisions: engine-first language, scale-to-zero by default, Azure to AWS migration, private-endpoint infra, research-preview tier model, human-in-the-loop over autonomy.

## Writing

- Server-Side Tool Search for a 62-Tool MCP Platform (2026.04) - https://substack.com/home/post/p-195517589
- Why Most AI Pilots Fail: The Missing Infrastructure Layer (2026.02)
- Scaling MCP: Lessons from 122M Compounds (2026.02)
- From Conversational to Computational AI (2026.02)

Writing available at: https://substack.com/@ariharrison

## Leadership decisions

Selected product, infra, and positioning calls made shipping NovoMCP. Posted in full at https://ariharrisonlab.github.io/#leadership.

- Two MCP servers, not one: governance separation between free-tier core tools and paid-only GPU/quantum. Different API key prefixes so a leaked core key can't burn compute budget.
- Scale-to-zero by default: every compute service ships with min_replicas=0; only public-path surfaces stay warm. Unit economics stay honest day one.
- "Engine" never "platform": locked public language to "computational chemistry engine"; positioning rule rather than positioning deck.
- Killed the campaign / autonomous-run system. Replaced with human-in-the-loop via MCP elicitation at every funnel stage. The audit log is the quality gate.
- Free research-preview tier, manually reviewed, with an auto-firing tier bump on signup. Trade-off: review labor. Buys product-market-fit signal and a referral surface.
- Private-endpoint-only infra (EKS API, Aurora, Redis, internal ALBs). Build on GH-hosted runners, deploy via OIDC -> SSM -> bastion. Pattern applied uniformly across every service.
